title: rhododendron viscosum var. serrulatum
intro: Rhododendron viscosum var. serrulatum, also known as the sticky azalea, is a variety of shrub native to North America. It is a deciduous shrub with fragrant white flowers and sticky leaves, which have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe digestive problems. Rhododendron viscosum var. serrulatum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used to support immune function, reduce stress, and promote overall well-being.
